---
title: Page templates
description: Diátaxis layouts for individual MDX pages — tutorial, how-to, reference, and explanation.
---

These are **page templates** — layouts for individual `.mdx` files, not site themes, starter repos, or `docs init` scaffolds. Pick the template that matches what the reader needs: a first walkthrough, one specific task, lookup facts, or background on *why*.

Each template includes a copy-paste skeleton, tone guidance, and a worked example using a fictional **Coffee Shop Ordering API** so you can compare how structure and voice change across documentation types.

## Choose a template

<CardGroup cols={2}>
  <Card title="Tutorial" icon="list-ol" href="/templates/tutorial">
    First-time walkthrough for one thing the reader sets out to do.
  </Card>
  <Card title="How-to" icon="wrench" href="/templates/how-to">
    One specific task for readers who already know the basics.
  </Card>
  <Card title="Reference" icon="book" href="/templates/reference">
    Lookup pages for APIs, config fields, commands, and exact syntax.
  </Card>
  <Card title="Explanation" icon="lightbulb" href="/templates/explanation">
    Background on why something works — architecture, intent, and tradeoffs.
  </Card>
</CardGroup>
